headline: Trump's threat to blockade Strait of Hormuz leaves more questions than answers
description: Doubts linger over whether the US can actually filter access to the strait, even as Washington claims that two of its destroyers have already managed to break through the Iranian blockade.

headline: In Hungary, a welcome defeat for illiberalism

Lafarge ex-CEO sentenced to six years in prison for funding jihadists in Syria
description: The French company, since acquired by the Swiss group Holcim, and former executives were charged with paying several million euros to armed jihadist groups in 2013–2014.

headline: 'Enough of war!': Pope Leo XIV publicly opposes Trump, who fiercely criticizes him in return
description: The head of the Catholic Church, who has repeatedly called for an end to the conflicts in Iran and Lebanon, has received sustained criticism from the US president.

headline: 'Lebanon's resilience is celebrated, as if survival were admirable rather than imposed'
description: Young Lebanese journalist Tayma Saliba writes about being from a country shaped by conflict, where war has infiltrated conversations for generations, in an op-ed for Le Monde.

headline: Parenting: Why the morning rush drives us crazy
description: Le Monde's parenting columnist, Clara Georges, breaks down one of her typically hectic family mornings, drawing on the insights of philosopher Pierre Vesperini and the solutions proposed by feminist author Titiou Lecoq.

In Algeria, the pope follows in the footsteps of the country's Christian history and reaches out to Muslims
description: On Monday, Leo XIV will become the first pontiff in history to visit Algeria, birthplace of the renowned Christian philosopher Saint Augustine. It was in Hippo, now Annaba, that the theologian wrote his most significant works in the fourth century.

headline: 'Do you have any regrets about having to kill?' On Trans-Siberian Railway, Russian soldiers recount time in Ukraine
description: 'On Board the Trans-Siberian' (2/2). As the train speeds toward the Russian Far East, between Novosibirsk and Vladivostok, the brutality of the war in Ukraine is at the center of conversations between soldiers on leave and 'ordinary' passengers.

headline: 'I stared death in the face!' On board the Trans-Siberian, a 9,000-kilometer journey through an exhausted Russia
description: 'On Board the Trans-Siberian' (1/2). In late December 2025 and early 2026, on the train linking Moscow to Vladivostok, soldiers returning from Ukraine cross paths with cautious travelers, careful about what they say. Beyond the Urals, propaganda posters attempt by every possible means to recruit new soldiers.

headline: 'Far from modernizing Hungary, Orban's economic policy has led the country into a dead end'
